title: Severe respiratory distress requiring immediate intervention

result.treatment_plan: Immediate respiratory support with mechanical ventilation if needed. Airway management and oxygen therapy. ICU admission mandatory. Consider ARDS protocol.
result.note: Patient presents with severe respiratory compromise requiring immediate life-saving intervention.

result.treatment_plan: Immediate cardiac resuscitation. Establish IV access, administer vasopressors if needed. Continuous cardiac monitoring. Emergency cardiology consultation. Prepare for defibrillation if needed.
result.note: Patient presents with life-threatening cardiac emergency requiring immediate resuscitation.

title: Severe sepsis or septic shock

result.treatment_plan: Immediate broad-spectrum antibiotics within first hour. Fluid resuscitation with crystalloids (30ml/kg bolus). Vasopressors if hypotensive despite fluids. Source control. Lactate monitoring.
result.note: Patient presents with severe sepsis or septic shock requiring immediate intervention according to Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Surviving Sepsis Campaign

title: Neurological emergency - decreased level of consciousness

result.treatment_plan: Immediate neurological assessment. Secure airway if GCS < 8. CT head to rule out hemorrhage. Monitor for herniation signs. NPO status. Consider ICP monitoring.
result.note: Patient presents with neurological emergency requiring immediate evaluation and possible neurosurgical intervention.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Neurocritical Care

title: Trauma with hemodynamic instability

result.treatment_plan: Immediate hemorrhage control. Massive transfusion protocol. Fluid resuscitation. Blood transfusion as needed. Surgical consultation for source control. Consider REBOA if appropriate.
result.note: Patient presents with traumatic injury requiring immediate surgical evaluation and possible operative intervention.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Trauma Care

title: Post-operative critical care required

result.treatment_plan: Close post-operative monitoring. Pain management with multimodal approach. Prevention of complications (DVT prophylaxis, antibiotic stewardship). Routine ICU care protocols. Wound care.
result.note: Patient requires post-operative critical care monitoring following major surgery.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Postoperative Care

title: Acute respiratory failure - non-critical

result.treatment_plan: Oxygen therapy with target SpO2 88-92% for COPD, >94% for others. Consider non-invasive ventilation (BiPAP) if indicated. Treat underlying cause. Bronchodilators for COPD/Asthma. Chest physiotherapy.
result.note: Patient presents with respiratory failure requiring close monitoring and supportive care with possible ventilatory support.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Respiratory Care

title: Acute coronary syndrome - stable

result.treatment_plan: Dual antiplatelet therapy (Aspirin + P2Y12 inhibitor). Anticoagulation (Heparin). Beta-blockers for rate control. High-intensity statins. Cardiology consultation for urgent angiogram. Monitor for arrhythmias.
result.note: Patient presents with acute coronary syndrome requiring urgent cardiac workup and possible revascularization.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Cardiac Care

title: Diabetic emergency - DKA or HHS

result.treatment_plan: Fluid resuscitation with Normal Saline (0.9% NaCl). Insulin drip 0.1 units/kg/hr. Potassium replacement when K+ < 5.2. Monitor glucose q1h. Monitor anion gap. Treat underlying trigger.
result.note: Patient presents with diabetic emergency requiring metabolic management and close electrolyte monitoring.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Metabolic Emergencies

title: Gastrointestinal emergency - bleeding or perforation

result.treatment_plan: Fluid resuscitation. Blood transfusion if hemoglobin < 7 g/dL (or < 8 if cardiac). NPO status. IV proton pump inhibitor high dose. GI surgery consultation. Consider endoscopy within 24 hours.
result.note: Patient presents with GI emergency requiring urgent intervention and possible surgical management.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - GI Emergencies

result.treatment_plan: Fluid management balancing perfusion and overload. Avoid nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, contrast, aminoglycosides). Monitor electrolytes closely (K+, Na+, Ca++, phosphate). Consider renal replacement therapy if indicated (uremic symptoms, hyperkalemia, acidosis, volume overload).
result.note: Patient requires ICU monitoring for acute kidney injury with possible need for dialysis.

result.treatment_plan: Supportive care (ABCs). Decontamination if appropriate (activated charcoal if within 1 hour of ingestion). Specific antidotes if known toxin (N-acetylcysteine for acetaminophen, naloxone for opioids, flumazenil for benzodiazepines). Monitor for complications. Consider hemodialysis for dialyzable toxins.
result.note: Patient presents with possible overdose requiring toxicology workup, supportive care, and possible antidote administration.

title: Electrolyte disturbance - severe

result.treatment_plan: Correct electrolyte abnormalities per protocol. Monitor cardiac rhythm continuously. Identify and treat underlying cause. For hyperkalemia: Calcium gluconate, Insulin+Glucose, Albuterol, Consider dialysis. For hyponatremia: Hypertonic saline if severe symptomatic.
result.note: Patient presents with severe electrolyte disturbance requiring ICU monitoring and careful correction.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Electrolyte Management

title: Pulmonary embolism

result.treatment_plan: Anticoagulation with heparin (UFH or LMWH). Consider thrombolysis (tPA) if hemodynamic unstable (systolic BP < 90). Investigate source of embolism with ultrasound. Consider IVC filter if contraindication to anticoagulation.
result.note: Patient presents with pulmonary embolism requiring ICU monitoring and anticoagulation therapy.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Venous Thromboembolism

title: Acute pancreatitis - severe

result.treatment_plan: NPO status. Aggressive fluid resuscitation with crystalloids. Pain management. Monitor for SIRS and organ dysfunction. Consider enteral nutrition if able. Antibiotics only if infected necrosis suspected. ERCP if biliary obstruction.
result.note: Patient presents with acute pancreatitis requiring ICU monitoring for potential complications.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - GI Disorders

result.treatment_plan: Monitor mental status closely. Lactulose for hepatic encephalopathy. rifaximin. Correct electrolyte abnormalities. NPO if aspiration risk. Consider albumin dialysis (MARS) if indicated. Evaluate for liver transplantation.
result.note: Patient presents with liver failure and possible hepatic encephalopathy requiring ICU monitoring.

title: Anaphylaxis

result.treatment_plan: Immediate IM Epinephrine 0.3-0.5 mg (1:1000). Call for help. Airway management (consider early intubation). IV fluids. Antihistamines. Corticosteroids. Monitor for biphasic reaction.
result.note: Patient presents with anaphylaxis requiring immediate life-saving intervention.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - Allergy Emergency

result.treatment_plan: Protect airway. IV Lorazepam 0.1 mg/kg or Diazepam. Load with Levetiracetam or Valproate. Consider phenytoin or phenobarbital if refractory. Maintain sedation if needed. EEG monitoring. Identify and treat underlying cause.
result.note: Patient presents with status epilepticus requiring immediate seizure termination and ICU monitoring.

title: ICU observation - general

result.treatment_plan: Close monitoring of vital signs every 4 hours. Supportive care. Regular reassessment of patient condition. Treat underlying condition. Fall precautions. DVT prophylaxis.
result.note: Patient requires ICU monitoring for ongoing evaluation and management of medical condition.

source: ICU Treatment Protocol v1 - General Observation
